atoms.semantic_claim: The old pending-block helper explicitly initializes the new `extra_data` next-block environment field to `None` when building pending block attributes.

destinations.evidence: Current pending env builder sets `extra_data: parent.extra_data().clone()`.
Current `NextBlockEnvAttributes.extra_data` is a required `Bytes` value, so the old optional `None`/no-override value cannot be represented in this type.
The next-block execution context forwards `attributes.extra_data` into the block execution context.
Block assembly writes `ctx.extra_data` into the assembled block header, so pending-block RPC header metadata can differ when the parent has non-default extraData.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Definitive classification: confirmed non-FSM pending-block metadata/API difference. The current code copies parent header extraData into locally derived pending blocks, while the old downstream atom explicitly used `extra_data: None` in the optional next-block attributes. This does not alter transaction selection/execution FSM, but it can change `eth_getBlockByNumber("pending")` header `extraData` when parent extraData differs from the intended default/no-override value.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build a derived pending block over a parent with distinct non-empty extraData and compare pending header extraData against the old optional-None/no-override behavior.
atoms.notes: Review closed as confirmed non-FSM API/metadata gap; no ambiguity remains.

summary: Ambiguity resolved: pending-block extraData is a confirmed non-FSM metadata/API difference, not an execution FSM bug.
notes: Current NextBlockEnvAttributes cannot represent the old optional `None` no-override value.
The value reaches assembled pending block headers through context_for_next_block and EthBlockAssembler.
This is user-facing pending-block header metadata, not transaction execution FSM.
